concern_reasons.note: Production code sets truncated=true when limit>cap regardless of actual hit count; test asserts reimplemented predicate not production expression
AC3 tests field shape only, not rendered stdout columns
real handle_query/run_serve path has zero integration test coverage
tests/mocks/ac6.rs is dead (not wired); should be deleted

falsification.test_audit: AC2 snippet truncation and AC4 read-only invariant are well-covered. AC1/AC5/AC7 tests are honest and verify the stated behaviors. AC2 truncated-flag and AC3 output-format tests are shallow — they assert reimplemented logic or field shape rather than the production behavior.
falsification.panic_audit: No unwrap/expect/panic in src/ on the happy path. SystemTime operations use .context(). The only expect() is in test helpers and is expected. Clean.
falsification.unsafe_audit: No unsafe present. #![deny(unsafe_code)] in both lib.rs and main.rs. deny_unsafe: true honored throughout.
falsification.public_api_audit: limit=usize::MAX is safe (clamped by min). Malformed recall JSON returns Err (not panic). No UB reachable on the public API surface.
falsification.deps_audit: 11 deps (≤ max_deps 12). All mainstream crates. futures-util added legitimately for StreamExt on async-nats Subscriber. No new advisory crates.
falsification.drift_audit: iter-1 diff is API-compat/clippy/test fixes only. Cargo.toml/clippy.toml/rust-toolchain.toml are expected config changes. No scope drift from PRD.

counter_attack.description: A test that calls handle_query with limit=100/cap=50 and a stub recall returning only 3 hits would expose the truncated=true-when-nothing-clamped bug.
counter_attack.test_skeleton: #[test] fn would_break_truncated_flag_when_few_hits() { /* limit=100, cap=50, recall returns 3 hits; truncated should be false but production code sets it true */ unimplemented!() }
